output:
I’m reviewing story `21-1-extract-workflow-actors` at the patch level first, then I’ll verify behavior against the surrounding module boundaries and test coverage. After that I’ll summarize findings by severity, including any AC gaps if the story isn’t fully evidenced by tests.I found the story commit and the current implementation footprint. Next I’m comparing `8d7a529` against the current `src/lib` layout and the story’s explicit acceptance checks, because this story is mostly a refactor and the main risk is hidden behavioral drift or uncovered boundaries.I’ve hit the main architectural seam now: the extraction itself is small, but I’m checking whether the new `workflow-types.ts` pulled the monolith back in through type imports or dropped any exported surface that callers relied on. That kind of “refactor-only” regression won’t show up in the line-count ACs alone.The circular-type concern looks better in the current tree than in the original story commit: `workflow-types.ts` now owns `EngineConfig`/`EngineEvent`, so I’m re-checking for a different class of regression instead of assuming the earlier patch diff is the final state. I’m now validating the exported API, the actual AC evidence, and whether the “no behavior change” claim is borne out by tests.I found a more concrete regression vector than the old type-cycle: the story commit originally preserved a wider re-export surface from `workflow-machine.ts`, but the current tree has already narrowed that surface. I’m checking whether that was intentional cleanup covered by tests, or an accidental compatibility break introduced while finishing the story.I have enough code context. I’m switching from static inspection to verification now: build, the story’s structural checks, and the dispatch/null-targeted test slices. If one of those contradicts the “pure extraction” claim, that will drive the verdict.The quick structural checks are clean so far: lint passed, build and type-check are still running, and the targeted vitest slices are in flight. I’m waiting on those because they’re the strongest evidence for whether this stayed a behavior-preserving extraction.The targeted coverage ACs passed exactly at the thresholds in the story: `dispatch` hit 11 files / 140 tests, and `null` hit 66 files / 254 tests. I’m doing one last pass with the full vitest run because AC2 is explicit about whole-suite regression safety, not just the focused slices.No findings.

`workflow-actors` is cleanly separated in [workflow-actors.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts), with shared boundary types in [workflow-types.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-types.ts) and constants in [workflow-constants.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-constants.ts). [workflow-machine.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts) now consumes the extracted logic without pulling runtime dependencies back into the actor module, so I did not find correctness regressions, security issues, or architecture violations in the current implementation.

AC coverage is evidenced in the current tree:
- `npm run build`: passed
- `npx vitest run`: `190` files, `4976` tests passed
- `npx vitest run -t 'dispatch'`: `11` files, `140` tests passed
- `npx vitest run -t 'null'`: `66` files, `254` tests passed
- `src/lib/workflow-actors.ts`: `199` lines
- `src/lib/workflow-machine.ts`: `1107` lines
- `grep -c 'workflow-machine' src/lib/workflow-actors.ts`: `0`
- `npx eslint src/lib/workflow-actors.ts`: passed
- `npx tsc --noEmit | grep workflow-actors`: no matches

Residual risk is low and limited to normal refactor risk around downstream consumers outside this repo’s test surface, but nothing in-repo indicates a break.

<verdict>pass</verdict>
